dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the feud just turned violent. We're running out of time for peace.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A patrol crossed the river and vandalized the other's grain stores. Now both sides are talking retaliation. {target_npc} was caught carrying a message and accused of being a spy. {quest_focus} is escalating beyond words. I need you to find {target_npc} and convince them to vouch for the bridge plan. And I need more {quest_item} to reinforce the foundations: if we build it, it has to be strong enough that nobody tears it down.
dialogueChunks.outro: They arrested the one person brave enough to carry peace. {quest_stakes} unless we act before the next confrontation.

playerResponses.text: I'll find {target_npc} and bring them home. Then we build that bridge.
Vandalism and arrests. This feud is a wildfire. But I'll try.
When both sides want war, one bridge won't stop them.

dialogueChunks.intro: The council raised the stakes, {player_name}. The task just got harder.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The elder council heard I had outside help and declared the trial invalid. They've set a new challenge: clear the {enemy_type} nest threatening the northern road {location_hint}. {target_npc} protested but was overruled. {quest_threat}: they want me to fail so they have a reason to send me away. If I clear that nest and bring back {quest_item} as proof, even the most stubborn elder can't deny I've earned my place.
dialogueChunks.outro: They keep moving the goal line. But {quest_stakes}, and I've come too far to walk away.

playerResponses.text: They want impossible? Let's give them impossible and make it look easy.
Moving the goal line is the oldest trick. We'll clear it anyway.
They'll just set another test after this one. It's not worth it.

dialogueChunks.intro: My family found out, {player_name}. There was a confrontation.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My father discovered the letters. He confronted the other family and words were exchanged that can't be taken back. {quest_focus} has exploded into open hostility. The ruin where {quest_origin} is kept has been overrun by {enemy_type}. I need you to fight through and retrieve both pieces, plus {quest_item} to restore the heirloom. If I present it at the next community gathering, reunited and whole, it might break through the anger.
dialogueChunks.outro: Words spoken in fury built these walls. Maybe an act of unity can tear them down. But {quest_stakes} if we don't act soon.

playerResponses.text: A reunited heirloom at a community gathering. That's a powerful statement. I'll get it.
Angry families and monster-filled ruins. At least the monsters are straightforward.
Your families are at war. A trinket won't fix that.
